meta.deck: A visibility-first read of where Northwind can watch an attacker move — and where the room is still dark.
meta.subtitle: Hybrid · no OT/ICS · deploys ML underwriting

adversaryDeck: Mid-market insurers sit squarely in the eCrime ransomware lane, and the intrusions are **identity-first** — stolen sessions and MFA abuse, not malware on the wire.

adversaries.name: Ransomware affiliates (eCrime)
adversaries.why: Carriers hold dense PII and pay to restore operations fast — a top eCrime target in 2025–26. Entry is increasingly through stolen identity rather than email payloads.

adversaries.name: Business email compromise operators
adversaries.why: Claims and premium flows make insurers a high-value BEC target; AiTM phishing defeats basic MFA and pivots to mailbox rules.

otherPriorities.title: Recent sector incident
otherPriorities.body: A peer Midwest carrier disclosed a ransomware event in Q1 2026 — public reporting points to **identity** as the entry point, not an endpoint payload.

assumptions: Hybrid environment with cloud-first identity (Entra/Okta). No OT/ICS in scope.
Deploys ML for underwriting — MITRE ATLAS is adjacent but out of scope for this visibility map.
No verified public breach was found for Northwind itself; sector and region data are used as a proxy and labeled as such.
Current-state log sources are an opening guess — correct them with the in-report toggles and the map will recolor.
